title: Europe’s Debt Burden in 2026: Which EU Countries Are Under the Most Pressure?
seoTitle: Europe’s Debt Burden in 2026: EU Countries Under the Most Pressure
summary: Europe’s public debt is not spread evenly. This data-backed analysis explains why total debt and debt-to-GDP tell two different stories about fiscal pressure in the EU.
excerpt: Europe’s public debt is not spread evenly. This data-backed analysis explains why total debt and debt-to-GDP tell two different stories about fiscal pressure in the EU.

body:
Europe’s debt burden is not spread evenly. Some countries owe more in absolute terms, while others face heavier pressure because their debt is large compared with the size of their economy.

Key takeaways

  • The EU government debt-to-GDP ratio stood at 81.7% at the end of 2025, according to Eurostat.
  • Total debt and debt-to-GDP measure different kinds of pressure.
  • Large economies can carry very large debts, but high debt ratios can create greater fiscal vulnerability.
  • The next Eurostat quarterly government debt update is scheduled for 21 July 2026.
